name: son-of-anton
description: Agentic IDE capabilities for Son of Anton

chatParticipants.name: anton
chatParticipants.description: AI orchestrator — routes requests to specialist agents

commands.name: plan
commands.description: Create an execution plan for a request

commands.name: approve
commands.description: Approve and execute the current plan

chatParticipants.name: anton-code
chatParticipants.description: Code generation specialist — writes and modifies code

commands.name: generate
commands.description: Generate code for a specific task

chatParticipants.name: anton-test
chatParticipants.description: Test writing specialist — generates comprehensive tests

chatParticipants.name: anton-security
chatParticipants.description: Security analysis specialist — scans for vulnerabilities

commands.name: audit
commands.description: Full security audit of changed files

chatParticipants.name: anton-docs
chatParticipants.description: Documentation specialist — generates and updates docs

chatParticipants.name: anton-e2e
chatParticipants.description: E2E test specialist — generates browser-based end-to-end tests

commands.name: e2e
commands.description: Generate E2E tests for user flows

chatParticipants.name: anton-ci
chatParticipants.description: CI/CD specialist — monitors pipelines and fixes failures

chatParticipants.name: anton-pr
chatParticipants.description: PR generation specialist — creates merge-ready pull requests

commands.name: pr

chatParticipants.name: anton-moderniser
chatParticipants.description: Legacy code modernisation specialist — systematically brings old code up to standard

commands.description: Start modernising a legacy module (US spelling)

commands.name: next-phase
commands.description: Advance to the next modernisation phase

chatParticipants.name: anton-spec
chatParticipants.description: Spec pipeline specialist — generates requirements, designs, and tasks from natural language

commands.name: spec
commands.description: Start a new spec pipeline from a feature description

commands.name: requirements
commands.description: Generate EARS requirements from a description

commands.name: design
commands.description: Generate technical design from approved requirements

commands.name: tasks
commands.description: Generate implementation tasks from approved design

commands.title: Anton: Sign In to ChatGPT / Codex
Anton: Sign Out of All Providers
Son of Anton: Show Harness Stats

sota.personality.enabled.description: Enable Son of Anton personality surfaces (Silicon Valley quotes, ASCII art, easter eggs). Disable to silence all flavour text and animations.

sota.chat.includeWorkspaceContext.description: Automatically include workspace context (active editor, README, recent files) in chat requests.

sota.chat.autoApproveSafeOperations.deprecationMessage: Replaced by the per-category auto-approval flags (`sota.autoApprove.read`, `sota.autoApprove.write`, `sota.autoApprove.shell`, `sota.autoApprove.mcp`). The legacy value is mirrored into `sota.autoApprove.read` on first run; please migrate when convenient.
sota.chat.autoApproveSafeOperations.markdownDescription: Legacy single-toggle auto-approval — kept for backward compatibility. The Phase 86 governance panel under Settings → Features replaces this with per-category toggles. **Use `sota.autoApprove.*` for new configuration.**

sota.autoApprove.read.markdownDescription: Auto-approve **read-only** tool calls (`read_file`, `list_directory`, `search_workspace`, `fetch_url`). On by default — these tools cannot mutate your workspace. Disable to require explicit approval for every read.

sota.autoApprove.write.markdownDescription: Auto-approve **file-write** tool calls (`write_file`, `edit_file`). **Off by default.** When enabled, Anton may overwrite or create files without asking. Pair with workspace trust + version control before turning this on.

sota.autoApprove.shell.markdownDescription: Auto-approve **shell command** tool calls (`run_command`). **Off by default.** Even when enabled, commands matching `sota.commandDenylist` still require explicit approval.

sota.autoApprove.mcp.markdownDescription: Auto-approve tool calls exposed by **Model Context Protocol** servers. **Off by default.** Per-server fine-grained toggles live separately in the MCP sub-tab.

sota.commandDenylist.markdownDescription: Regex patterns (one per entry) that force explicit approval for `run_command` calls even when `sota.autoApprove.shell` is enabled. Anton matches the full reconstructed command line (executable + args, joined by spaces) against each pattern; a hit blocks auto-approval.

sota.spendLimit.session.markdownDescription: Per-session USD spend cap. When the session's cumulative cost exceeds this number, the next chat turn is blocked with a modal. Increase the cap or start a new conversation to reset. Advisory unless `sota.spendLimit.enabled` is `true`.

sota.spendLimit.task.markdownDescription: Per-task USD spend cap. When a single user turn (one message → all subtasks → all tool calls) exceeds this cost, Anton aborts mid-turn. Use `/continue` to retry once the cap is raised. Advisory unless `sota.spendLimit.enabled` is `true`.

sota.spendLimit.enabled.markdownDescription: Enforce session and per-task spend caps. When `false`, the meters still surface in the UI but never block.

sota.chat.focusChainEnabled.markdownDescription: Show a focus-chain checklist beside long agent runs. Advisory — runtime wiring is in flight.

sota.chat.perMessageHoverDetails.markdownDescription: Surface a hover popover on each chat message with token / model / latency details. Advisory.

sota.chat.personaAccents.markdownDescription: Tint message bubbles with the assigned persona's accent colour. Advisory — disable for a neutral colour scheme.

sota.personality.voiceIntensity.markdownDescription: How strongly Anton's deadpan voice colours its responses. 0 = strictly utilitarian. 10 = full Silicon Valley. Advisory.

sota.personality.asciiArt.markdownDescription: Show ASCII art on idle / loading screens. Advisory.

sota.personality.easterEggs.markdownDescription: Surface easter eggs (e.g. the git-blame command jokes). Advisory.

sota.personality.antonIsWatching.markdownDescription: Anton occasionally surfaces a dry observation while you work. Fires at most once per editor window, only after recent activity, and never while you're idle.

sota.personality.antonIsWatching.frequen.markdownDescription: How often Anton's observations surface. `rare` = 2–4h window, `normal` = 30min–4h window, `often` = 10min–2h window.

sota.terminal.outputLineCap.markdownDescription: Maximum lines of terminal output Anton ingests per attachment. Advisory — the runtime cap is enforced separately.

sota.terminal.shellIntegration.markdownDescription: Enable VS Code shell integration when capturing terminal output. Advisory.

sota.reasoningEffort.markdownDescription: Reasoning effort sent to gpt-5 / o-series reasoning models. Ignored for capability-incompatible models.

sota.thinkingBudgetTokens.markdownDescription: Extended thinking budget (tokens) for Claude 4.x models. 0 disables thinking. Ignored for non-Anthropic models.

sota.checkpoints.enabled.markdownDescription: Capture a workspace checkpoint (via `git stash create`) on every chat turn so you can roll the working tree back to any prior state. Disable to opt out of the per-turn capture overhead — existing checkpoints remain restorable.

sota.checkpoints.maxCount.markdownDescription: Maximum number of checkpoints retained across all conversations. Once exceeded, the oldest checkpoints are pruned. Note: the underlying git stash commits become dangling references that git's reachability GC reaps after 30 days.

sota.trustedFolders.markdownDescription: Workspaces where Son of Anton has been granted trust to run tools and modify code. Edit via the **Son of Anton: Manage Trusted Folders** command, or hand-edit this list to audit / pre-bless folders. Paths added here are merged with entries persisted via the in-product trust prompt; removing a path here does **not** implicitly revoke trust — use **Son of Anton: Revoke Workspace Trust** for that.

sota.completions.debounceMs.description: Debounce time in milliseconds before triggering completions

sota.traces.maxSessions.description: Maximum number of trace sessions to retain

sota.apiKey.description: Anthropic API key (prefer environment variable ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)

sota.agents.anton.model.markdownDescription: Model id used by the orchestrator (`@anton`). Leave empty to use the default (`opus`). Common picks: `opus`, `sonnet`, `claude-opus-4-7`, `claude-sonnet-4-7`, `gpt-5`, `gpt-4-1`. The chat composer's per-turn picker still overrides this on each request. Reload the window after changing.

sota.agents.anton-code.model.markdownDescription: Model id used by `@anton-code` (code generation). Default `sonnet`. Reload the window after changing.

sota.agents.anton-test.model.markdownDescription: Model id used by `@anton-test` (test writing). Default `sonnet`. Reload the window after changing.

sota.agents.anton-e2e.model.markdownDescription: Model id used by `@anton-e2e` (E2E test generation). Default `sonnet`. Reload the window after changing.

sota.agents.anton-security.model.markdownDescription: Model id used by `@anton-security` (vulnerability scanning). Default `sonnet`. Reload the window after changing.

sota.agents.anton-docs.model.markdownDescription: Model id used by `@anton-docs` (documentation). Default `haiku` (cheap + fast). Reload the window after changing.

sota.agents.anton-ci.model.markdownDescription: Model id used by `@anton-ci` (CI failure analysis). Default `sonnet`. Reload the window after changing.

sota.agents.anton-pr.model.markdownDescription: Model id used by `@anton-pr` (PR generation). Default `sonnet`. Reload the window after changing.

sota.agents.anton-moderniser.model.markdownDescription: Model id used by `@anton-moderniser` (legacy code modernisation). Default `sonnet`. Reload the window after changing.

sota.agents.anton-review.model.markdownDescription: Model id used by the review agent (runs after every specialist subtask). Default `sonnet`. Use `haiku` to make reviews cheaper at the cost of some signal. Reload the window after changing.

sota.openaiApiKey.markdownDescription: OpenAI API key used for ChatGPT / Codex models when no OAuth provider is connected. Get one from [platform.openai.com](https://platform.openai.com/api-keys). Falls back to the `OPENAI_API_KEY` environment variable when this is empty.

sota.foundryApiKey.markdownDescription: API key for Microsoft Foundry / Azure OpenAI. Get it from the Azure portal: navigate to your Azure OpenAI resource → Keys and Endpoint. Falls back to `AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY` or `FOUNDRY_API_KEY` env var when this is empty.

sota.foundryEndpoint.markdownDescription: Base endpoint for your Microsoft Foundry / Azure OpenAI resource, e.g. `https://my-resource.openai.azure.com` (no trailing slash). Found in the Azure portal under Keys and Endpoint.

sota.foundryApiVersion.markdownDescription: Foundry / Azure OpenAI API version. See Microsoft's [API reference](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/ai-services/openai/reference) for current values.

sota.foundryDeployments.markdownDescription: JSON object mapping Foundry model ids to deployment names. Example: `{ "foundry-gpt-4o": "gpt-4o-eastus", "foundry-gpt-4o-mini": "gpt-4o-mini", "foundry-claude-sonnet": "claude-sonnet-4-5" }`. Each deployment must already exist in your Foundry resource.

sota.foundryProfiles.markdownDescription: JSON object mapping a custom display name to a Foundry deployment profile: `{ "<name>": { "endpoint": "https://...", "deployment": "...", "apiVersion": "..." } }`. Surfaces in the model picker as `Foundry · <name>`. The legacy `sota.foundryDeployments` model→deployment map continues to work for the default profile.

sota.bedrockProfiles.markdownDescription: JSON object mapping a custom display name to a Bedrock credential profile: `{ "<name>": { "region": "us-east-1", "profile": "...", "accessKeyId": "...", "secretAccessKey": "..." } }`. Surfaces in the model picker as `Bedrock · <name>`. Use this to switch between regions or accounts mid-session.

sota.googleProfiles.markdownDescription: JSON object mapping a custom display name to a Google Gemini API key: `{ "<name>": { "apiKey": "...", "project": "..." } }`. Use this when juggling personal vs work Gemini quotas.

sota.bedrockRegion.markdownDescription: AWS region for Bedrock requests (e.g. `us-east-1`, `us-west-2`, `eu-west-3`). Each region exposes a different subset of foundation models — check Bedrock's model availability page.

sota.bedrockAccessKeyId.markdownDescription: AWS access key ID. Leave blank to use the standard AWS credential chain (env vars, shared credentials file, IAM role, etc.). Falls back to `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID` env var. **For long-lived credentials only — do not paste production access keys here unless this user-data dir is appropriately protected.**

sota.bedrockSecretAccessKey.markdownDescription: AWS secret access key. Leave blank to use the standard AWS credential chain. Falls back to `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` env var.

sota.bedrockSessionToken.markdownDescription: Optional AWS session token (for temporary credentials from STS). Falls back to `AWS_SESSION_TOKEN` env var.

sota.bedrockProfile.markdownDescription: Named profile from `~/.aws/credentials` to use. Takes precedence over the access-key settings above when set. Equivalent to `AWS_PROFILE` env var.

sota.bedrockModelMap.markdownDescription: JSON object mapping Son of Anton's Bedrock model ids to actual Bedrock model invocation IDs. Default mapping if empty: `bedrock-claude-sonnet` → `anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022-v2:0`, `bedrock-claude-haiku` → `anthropic.claude-3-5-haiku-20241022-v1:0`. Override here for region-specific or cross-region inference profiles.

sota.googleApiKey.markdownDescription: API key for the Google Gemini API. Get one from [aistudio.google.com](https://aistudio.google.com/app/apikey). Falls back to `GOOGLE_API_KEY` or `GEMINI_API_KEY` env var when this is empty. Note: this is the consumer Gemini API endpoint (`generativelanguage.googleapis.com`), not full Vertex AI — Vertex with ADC OAuth is a separate forthcoming integration.

sota.googleModelMap.markdownDescription: JSON object mapping Son of Anton's Gemini model ids to actual Google API model names. Default mapping if empty: `gemini-1-5-pro` → `gemini-1.5-pro`, `gemini-1-5-flash` → `gemini-1.5-flash`, `gemini-2-0-flash` → `gemini-2.0-flash-exp`. Override here to pin specific versions or use experimental endpoints.

sota.anthropicBaseUrl.markdownDescription: Override for the Anthropic API base URL. Empty falls back to `https://api.anthropic.com`. Useful for proxies, on-prem Helix, or regional endpoints.

sota.anthropicCustomHeaders.markdownDescription: JSON object of additional headers sent on every Anthropic request (e.g. `{ "Helix-Tenant": "..." }`). Auth + version headers always win on conflict.

sota.openaiBaseUrl.markdownDescription: Override for the OpenAI base URL. Empty falls back to `https://api.openai.com/v1`. Useful for OpenAI-compatible APIs (e.g. Together, Anyscale).

sota.openaiOrgId.markdownDescription: Optional OpenAI organisation id sent as the `OpenAI-Organization` header.

sota.openaiCustomHeaders.markdownDescription: JSON object of additional headers sent on every OpenAI request. Auth header always wins on conflict.

sota.foundryCustomHeaders.markdownDescription: JSON object of additional headers sent on every Foundry / Azure OpenAI request.

sota.bedrockEndpointUrl.markdownDescription: Optional Bedrock endpoint URL override (e.g. for VPC interface endpoints). Empty uses the default `bedrock-runtime.<region>.amazonaws.com`.

sota.geminiProjectId.markdownDescription: Optional GCP project id, used by future Vertex-AI endpoints. Today's consumer Gemini path ignores this.

sota.geminiLocation.markdownDescription: Optional GCP location/region for Vertex AI (e.g. `us-central1`). Today's consumer Gemini path ignores this.

sota.codeGraph.autoStart.description: Automatically start the bundled code-graph docker stack when this workspace opens. Toggled on by 'Son of Anton: Enable Code Graph'.

sota.codeGraph.backend.description: Which code-graph backend to use. 'auto' detects an existing Docker stack and falls back to the embedded MCP server. 'embedded' always spawns the bundled child process. 'docker' defers to the legacy services/{indexer,lsif,mcp-gateway} stack. 'off' disables the backend entirely.

sota.codeGraph.indexRoot.description: Root directory the code-graph indexer scans. Defaults to the active workspace folder when blank.

sota.codeGraph.embedder.description: Embedder used for semantic_search. 'none' returns empty results. 'local' downloads a ~30MB fastembed model on first call. 'provider' calls an OpenAI-compatible /embeddings endpoint (configure sota.codeGraph.providerEmbedder.*).

sota.codeGraph.providerEmbedder.endpoint.description: OpenAI-compatible /embeddings endpoint URL. Used when sota.codeGraph.embedder is 'provider'.

sota.codeGraph.providerEmbedder.model.description: Model identifier passed to the provider embedder endpoint.

sota.codeGraph.providerEmbedder.dims.description: Output dimension of the provider embedder. Must match the model's native dimension.

sota.mcp.servers.description: MCP servers to connect to over stdio. Each entry spawns a child process and exposes its tools as `mcp__<server>__<tool>` in the chat.

name.description: Unique server identifier (used as the prefix in tool names).

env.description: Environment variables for the spawned process.

cwd.description: Working directory for the child process. Defaults to the workspace root.

sotaAuth.anthropic-oauth.clientId.markdownDescription: OAuth client ID for the Claude (claude.ai) sign-in flow. **Most users should leave this blank and use an API key instead** — Anthropic does not currently offer public OAuth client registration for third-party tools. If you have been issued a client ID by Anthropic (e.g. as part of a partner program), paste it here. Otherwise set `#sota.apiKey#` to your Anthropic API key from [console.anthropic.com](https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys).

sotaAuth.chatgpt-oauth.clientId.markdownDescription: OAuth client ID for the ChatGPT / Codex (OpenAI) sign-in flow. **Most users should leave this blank and use an API key instead** — OpenAI does not currently offer a third-party OAuth program comparable to a developer console here. If you have been issued a client ID for OpenAI's PKCE flow, paste it here. Otherwise set `#sota.openaiApiKey#` to your OpenAI API key from [platform.openai.com](https://platform.openai.com/api-keys).
